description_long: This object is a horizontally positioned, freshly cut tree log with both ends cleanly sawn, revealing pale, smooth wood grain on the circular faces. The bark is mostly intact and covered with patches of green moss and climbing ivy, giving it a natural, organic appearance. Several small branches and twigs protrude from the log and are scattered around its base, which rests on a bed of dry leaves and forest debris. The log is cylindrical, with a diameter and length suggesting it was cut from a medium-sized tree. The surface of the bark is rough and textured, while the cut ends are smoother but still show some irregularities. There are no visible artificial modifications, handles, or reinforcements, and the log appears solid and heavy.
description: A freshly cut, moss- and ivy-covered tree log with rough bark and smooth sawn ends, resting on a bed of leaves and twigs.
description_view_0: The log's circular sawn end is prominent, with visible wood grain and mossy bark, surrounded by scattered twigs and leaves.
description_view_1: This view highlights the length of the log, showing more of the bark's texture and the dense ivy growth along its side.
description_view_2: The opposite sawn end is visible, with some discoloration and more leafy debris around the base.
description_view_3: The log is seen at an angle, emphasizing the rough bark, protruding branches, and a hollow or knot near the top.
